meta.title: Incremental Static Regeneration (ISR) Explained
meta.excerpt: The best of both worlds - combine static generation's speed with the ability to update content without rebuilding your entire application.

props.content: ISR allows you to create or update static pages after build time without rebuilding your entire site. Pages are regenerated in the background while serving stale content to users.

ISR combines the benefits of static generation (instant loads, CDN caching) with the ability to update content periodically. When a page is requested after the revalidation period, the cached version is served immediately while a new version is generated in the background.

leftItems: Blog posts with view counts or comments
Marketing pages with A/B tested content
rightItems: Content that must be immediately consistent
Content that never changes (use pure SSG)

props.content: Set a revalidation period in seconds for automatic periodic regeneration:

props.title: app/products/[id]/page.tsx

props.content: Control caching granularly at the fetch level for different data sources:

props.title: app/news/page.tsx

props.content: Trigger revalidation immediately when content changes via webhooks or manual triggers:

props.title: app/api/revalidate/route.ts
app/products/[id]/page.tsx

props.content: Choose revalidation times based on how frequently your content changes and how critical freshness is. Too short increases server load; too long serves stale content.

rows: Manual CMS updates, should be immediate

props.content: In our electrical services documentation, ISR would be ideal for:

features.title: Service pricing pages
features.description: Update prices without full redeploy using on-demand revalidation when content changes in Strapi

features.title: Team/staff pages
features.description: CMS-managed with on-demand revalidation when team information is updated

features.title: Blog/news section
features.description: Periodic updates with reasonable freshness (15-30 minute revalidation)

props.content: ISR gives you the performance benefits of static generation with the freshness of dynamic content. Use time-based revalidation for periodic updates and on-demand revalidation for immediate changes from your CMS.
